source: Sakana AI marketing chart (Fugu launch, June 2026)
provenance_note: Numbers chosen and arranged by the model's own maker. Conveniently omits Claude Mythos 5 (80.3) and Claude Fable 5 (80.0) — the two models that actually top SWE-bench Pro. See the independently-verified board below.

Fugu-Ultra.note: Sakana's own model. It's a router over a model pool, not a single LLM — scaffold, retry budget, and tool access are undisclosed. Not reproduced by neutral testers (remoteopenclaw, Jun 2026).
Fugu-Ultra.why: The headline act: a routing SYSTEM benchmarked as if it were a model. Interesting precisely because you can't tell how much of the score is the model vs the scaffold — the whole COI case study in one column.

Fugu.note: Same maker, same undisclosed scaffold, same lack of third-party reproduction.
Fugu.why: The latency-tuned little sibling. Same provenance problems, less to prove.

Opus 4.8.note: SWE-bench Pro 69.2 matches independent trackers (BenchLM.ai, Artificial Analysis). A competitor placed the number, but it checks out.
Opus 4.8.why: Tops the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (61.4) independently, yet Sakana's chart frames it as a loser by hiding the two Claudes above it. The gap between 'what the chart shows' and 'what independent trackers show' is the interesting part.

GPT-5.5.note: SWE-bench Pro 58.6 matches BenchLM.ai independent tracking.
GPT-5.5.why: Present mainly as a reference point; its numbers corroborate but it wins little in this framing.

Gemini 3.1.note: The independent SWE-bench Pro board lists 'Gemini 3.5 Flash' at 55.1 — no 'Gemini 3.1' at 54.2. Variant/label mismatch; treat the whole column as suspect.
Gemini 3.1.why: Interesting for the wrong reason: the column may not even be the model it claims to be. A labeling mismatch this basic taints every number under it.
